config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi, bukan ditompa oleh mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea makes me uneasy because history shows how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuses, discrimination, or government overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in practice, and there are real limits when one person’s freedom directly harms others, like public health or safety rules. So I’d say society matters, but it should be constrained by strong protections for individual rights, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu jika dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia label “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil atau minoritas. Memang ada keadaan tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keselamatan umum, ketika pembatasan hak individu bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan alasan bebas untuk membungkam. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang selalu mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak orang sambil mengurus kepentingan bersama secara adil. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua terus, yang paling sering rugi justru rakyat biasa, bukan elite.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general principle because in the United States, putting society first too easily turns into excuses for censorship, discrimination, or government overreach. The strongest version of a society is not one that steamrolls people, but one that protects individual rights while narrowly limiting them only when there is a clear, concrete harm to others, like in public health or safety. Agent A’s point about “the common good” being used against ordinary people is exactly why I am cautious about this language. If society’s interests always outrank individual rights, then rights stop being rights and become privileges the majority can revoke whenever it wants.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia istilah kepentingan masyarakat terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang kecil, minoritas, atau warga yang kritis. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu; pembatasan hanya layak kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi dengan ketat. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi biasanya bukan masyarakat yang lebih adil, melainkan mayoritas atau penguasa yang makin leluasa menekan yang lemah. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, lalu kepentingan umum diurus tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in the United States it has a bad habit of becoming a blank check for majorities or governments to override unpopular people. The other side is right that public health and safety can justify limits sometimes, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and reviewable, not a default assumption that society always wins. If individual rights are treated as second-class, they are no longer real rights at all. A free society should protect people from harm while still treating individual liberty as the starting point, not the exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
